description: The CodeBoarding architecture follows an agentic pipeline pattern where static analysis data is transformed into high-level architectural insights through LLM-driven reasoning. The process begins with environment orchestration and dependency management, moves into a multi-stage extraction phase where LSP-based static analysis and graph clustering define the code's structure, and culminates in an AI-powered synthesis layer that evaluates system health and generates visual documentation.

components.name: Orchestration & Infrastructure
components.description: System entry point and environment manager handling CLI parsing, repository lifecycle, and dependency registration.

components.name: Workflow Controller & CLI
components.description: Acts as the system's brain and primary entry point, parsing user intent and bootstrapping the execution environment.

components.name: Environment & Tooling Manager
components.description: Manages the physical infrastructure layer, including the lifecycle of external binaries like LSP servers and Node.js runtimes.

components.name: Repository & State Manager
components.description: Handles interaction with the target source code, detecting changes between commits and managing analysis persistence.

components.name: Discovery & Registry Framework
components.description: Provides extensibility and discovery mechanisms, maintaining a central registry of tools and repository structure.

components.name: Static Analysis & Graph Engine
components.description: Core engine for extracting symbols via LSP, building call graphs, and identifying logical code communities via clustering algorithms.

components.name: Analysis Orchestrator & Result Manager
components.description: Acts as the central controller for the analysis lifecycle. It manages incremental updates, merges results from multiple language-specific passes, and maintains the state of the analysis in a unified format.

components.name: LSP Infrastructure & Language Adapters
components.description: Manages the low-level complexities of the Language Server Protocol and environment-specific configurations. It handles JSON-RPC communication and provides a standardized interface for different programming languages.

components.name: Call Graph & Symbol Engine
components.description: The primary engine for structural analysis. It resolves symbols, builds edges between callers and callees, and maintains a symbol table, including fallback mechanisms like regex-based source inspection.

components.name: Graph Clustering & Community Detector
components.description: Applies mathematical clustering algorithms to the call graph to discover communities or logical modules, providing a hierarchical view of the codebase.

components.name: Agent Interface & Transformation Layer
components.description: Converts internal graph structures into external representations, generating LLM-friendly strings and providing tools for the Agent Core to query the codebase.

components.name: AI Reasoning & Health
components.description: The system's brain, using LLMs for semantic interpretation of clusters and performing architectural health checks.

components.name: Agentic Orchestration & Context Management
components.description: Central controller managing the lifecycle of AI agents, token budgeting, and the high-level reasoning flow from metadata extraction to synthesis.

components.name: Prompt Strategy & LLM Abstraction
components.description: Factory layer abstracting LLM providers to ensure reasoning tasks receive optimized, model-specific instructions.

components.name: Architectural Health & Quality Guard
components.description: Performs deterministic analysis to evaluate structural integrity and identify anti-patterns like circular dependencies.

components.name: Incremental State & Delta Engine
components.description: Manages analysis evolution by tracking codebase changes to ensure reasoning targets only modified components.

components.name: Agent Tooling & Repository Interface
components.description: Provides sensory tools for agents to interact with the physical codebase, including file system and structure inspection.

components_relations.relation: consumes health reports for semantic explanation
uses repository tools for dependency traversal

components.name: Synthesis & Output
components.description: Aggregates analysis, AI insights, and health metrics into user-facing documentation like Mermaid diagrams and HTML reports.

components.name: Analysis Orchestrator & State Manager
components.description: Aggregates disparate analysis results into a unified, persistent state and manages the lifecycle of analysis data.

components.name: Context Discovery & Pre-processor
components.description: Scans the project environment to establish structural boundaries, calculate change sets, and provide ground truth metadata.

components.name: Entity Hydration & Linker
components.description: Resolves high-level architectural components back to specific source code references, enriching models with file paths and method signatures.

components.name: Multi-Format Rendering Engine
components.description: The presentation layer that translates structural data into visual representations and structured documents for end-user consumption.

components_relations.relation: Supplies project structure and metadata to
Passes raw data to be enriched by

components.name: Knowledge & Telemetry
components.description: Manages state, observability, persistence, and execution metrics across the pipeline.

components.name: Knowledge Persistence Engine
components.description: Manages the long-term storage and retrieval of static analysis artifacts using content-addressable keys to enable incremental processing.

components.name: Execution & Resource Monitor
components.description: Provides real-time tracking of the pipeline's execution state, including step-level timing and LLM token consumption metrics.

components.name: Product Analytics & Identity
components.description: Captures high-level functional events and anonymized usage data to track tool effectiveness and user engagement.

components.name: Configuration & Capability Registry
components.description: Centralizes user settings and AI model constraints, acting as the policy engine for the entire pipeline.

components_relations.relation: Provides model-specific settings for cache key generation
Forwards aggregated execution summaries for event recording
Queries cache to determine execution skip or monitoring
Defines thresholds and streaming targets for monitoring
